title: When does 30 come out
wikitext:
[]: When does 3.0 come out? Post by mastervampire » Wed Jul 01, 2009 2:46 am So now that the second movie has been released, do we know when 3.0 will come out?

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jul 01, 2009 2:48 am By extrapolation, somewhere around Easter 2011.

[]: Post by Axell » Wed Jul 01, 2009 3:00 am end the of the mayan calender sounds about right

[]: Post by JimmyWolk » Wed Jul 01, 2009 3:02 am Mid 2008. Oh, wait...

[]: Post by mastervampire » Wed Jul 01, 2009 3:03 am 2011??! fk that

[]: Post by Axell » Wed Jul 01, 2009 3:10 am well do you want it done now or do you want it done right? the real question is when will 2.0 come to the states? and will it only play in canada again?

[]: Post by Combs » Wed Jul 01, 2009 3:12 am In my view, Canada's being the north american test market. If it flops, they aren't releasing it in the states. If it's win, expect a release in some 200 theatres. Edit: I'm expecting somewhere from september 2010 to Mr. Tine's prediction. Unless its still twinned with the last movie, then i'm thinking 2011 Christmastime

[]: Post by Legendary » Wed Jul 01, 2009 3:26 am Actually, it being twinned won't create a delay- the idea behind the twinning is to produce one full length feature film by combining 3.0 and Final, so they'd still need the same amount of animation as they would from just 3.0 if they seperated the two.

[]: Post by Timstuff » Wed Jul 01, 2009 3:42 am The next two movies are only going to be 45 minutes, correct? That's just about half as long a typical feature length animated film, so I imagine it should come out about twice as quickly as 2.0 did.

[]: Post by Heromaster111 » Wed Jul 01, 2009 3:53 am Timstuff wrote: The next two movies are only going to be 45 minutes, correct? That's just about half as long a typical feature length animated film, so I imagine it should come out about twice as quickly as 2.0 did.

[]: Post by JimmyWolk » Wed Jul 01, 2009 6:59 am Well, even if they are shown back to back, they still could be well over 45 minutes each. 2.0 was also announced as 90 minutes and ended up with 108(?), and let's not forget that there's been "small" discrepancies with the release dates too, which have been revealed about the same time. I have some hope (though I'd be far from betting on it) that they have worked a bit (more) in advance this time or at least don't waste anymore time and resources on even more variants of 1.0 and now 2.0, so that the next one (whether it will be the double feature or "just" 3.0) will be out a bit faster than almost 2 years.

[]: Post by BattleMonkey » Wed Jul 01, 2009 7:32 am Maybe it wont take as long since they likely are working on it back to back with 2.0. 1.0 was largely rehashed footage that simply was enhanced in various ways with bits of all new animation, 2.0 onward obviously are taking much more work in the animation department which could be the reason why 2.0 took longer than originally expected. Previews for 3.0 already being given would show they are working on it and hopefully we don't wait as long this time around for the next installment.

[]: Post by JimmyWolk » Wed Jul 01, 2009 7:51 am BattleMonkey wrote: Previews for 3.0 already being given would show they are working on it

[]: Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Jul 01, 2009 8:44 am But 2.0 is the one where the story started to diverge heavily from the series. I hope that finishing Ha took so long because the story went through numerous rewrites, not because of technical difficulties. Thus, in the long creative process, they may have figured out already how they want to conclude this tetralogy, and already have the majority of the storyboards and the general plots to be used in 3.0 and 4.0. What i'm trying to spew out is that i think the plot for 3.0 and 4.0 is already finished, and so completing them won't take as much time as 2.0. Of course, 2.0 still has to do it's theatrical run, and the DVD releases and stuff, so 3.0 won't be around the corner next Tuesday, but it might come out sooner then 2011. 


[]: Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Jul 01, 2009 10:25 am Anytime between Summer 2010 and Summer 2011. For some reason I believe the large gap between 1.0 and 2.0 was because Anno and crew really started to reshape their original plans for Rebuild. I feel a 2010 release is very possible but it all depends on how fast Anno and crew get to work (instead of dicking around with things like 2.01 or 2.11). I'd say if we don't hear any details about 3.0 before Dec 31, 2009- details like a vague release date or title- than 3.0 will come out Summer 2011. As for the U.S. release, I'd say late Fall 2010. I think you could set your calendar to one year after 1.0 hits DVD/Blu-Ray will be when 2.0 hits.

[]: Post by BattleMonkey » Wed Jul 01, 2009 10:41 am Better question is when will 2.5 be out.

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jul 01, 2009 10:42 am mastervampire wrote: 2011??! fk that

[]: Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Jul 01, 2009 11:15 am Well, if Kim "Dear Leader" succession goes wrong, don't think we'll ever get to see 3.0 (the first missile will be hitting the 4th floor, office 402, of certain building). That might be a relief in some sort of way.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 01, 2009 11:30 am I think we're done for now.
